name: Buttermilk, cultured lowfat (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (~100 mL, density ~1.03 g/mL); concentration_mM = (g/100g) x 10 / MW x 1000

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 170874 (total sugars 5.35 g/100g, essentially all lactose in dairy)
components.evidence: Dominant fermentable sugar in dairy; USDA reports total sugars 5.35 g/100g with lactose the sole disaccharide of milk. Partially fermented in culturing, so slightly below fresh-milk lactose (~4.8-5.0 g/100g).

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Inferred from lactose hydrolysis during LAB fermentation (Gebreselassie 2016, IJDT doi:10.1111/1471-0307.12236)
components.evidence: Released with glucose by microbial beta-galactosidase during culturing; present but not individually quantified in USDA. Typically Leuconostoc/Lactococcus consume glucose and leave residual galactose.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Monosaccharide liberated from lactose during fermentation and largely consumed; low residual, unquantified in USDA.

components.name: lactic acid (L+/D-)
components.source: Gebreselassie et al. 2016 IJDT doi:10.1111/1471-0307.12236 (5.35-11.56 mg/g, midpoint ~8 mg/g); titratable acidity 0.75-0.85% lactate
components.evidence: DEFINING organic acid of cultured buttermilk and a major carbon/energy source; produced from lactose by Lactococcus lactis. Range 0.53-1.16 g/100g depending on fermentation extent; pH ~4.4-4.7.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Gebreselassie 2016 doi:10.1111/1471-0307.12236 (52-1712 ug/g); starting milk citrate ~0.16 g/100g
components.evidence: Milk citrate (~0.15-0.18 g/100g) is largely fermented by Leuconostoc/citrate-positive Lactococcus to CO2, diacetyl and acetate; residual is low and variable. Report residual midpoint.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: By-product of heterofermentative citrate/lactose metabolism by Leuconostoc; present at low levels, contributes to aroma and acidity.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Bovine milk orotic acid up to ~6 mg/100 mL (dairy literature; Gebreselassie 2016 lists orotate)
components.evidence: Characteristic bovine-milk pyrimidine intermediate; survives fermentation. Range ~2-6 mg/100g.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.evidence: Central fermentation intermediate detected in buttermilk; trace, unquantified.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Minor LAB fermentation product; trace, unquantified.

components.name: protein (casein + whey)
components.evidence: Predominantly casein plus whey; source of the amino acid profile below (values are total protein-bound, not all free).

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Reported as cystine (dimer); mM as cystine MW 240.3.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: MISSING from current profile; added. Protein-derived.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: MISSING from current profile; added. Protein-derived.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (as glutamic acid); protein-derived.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-derived; current profile lists 'D-Serine' which is an error (L-serine is the proteinogenic form).

components.name: vitamin A (retinol)
components.evidence: 47 ug RAE/100g total; retinol from milk fat.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Minor provitamin A carotenoid in milk fat.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: USDA FDC 170874 (not detected in SR); milk biotin ~2-4 ug/100g in FRIDA/UK data
components.evidence: Present in milk though not quantified in this USDA record; keep with null amount.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Water-soluble essential nutrient present in milk.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion; USDA does not list but milk chloride is well characterized.

components.name: iodine
components.evidence: Characteristic dairy micronutrient; not in USDA SR record.

components.name: cobalt
components.evidence: Present essentially only as the cobalt centre of B12; keep as trace with null.

components.name: total fat (milk fat)
components.evidence: Lowfat product; saturated 0.55 g, MUFA 0.20 g, PUFA 0.03 g.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Dominant monounsaturated fatty acid of milk fat.

components.name: butyric acid (C4:0)
components.source: Milk fat characteristic short-chain FA (USDA milk fat profile)
components.evidence: Signature short-chain milk fatty acid; scaled small at 1.1 g total fat.

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine animal sterol of dairy; KEEP (buttermilk is an animal product).

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Food-preservative/antimicrobial additive, not an intrinsic buttermilk nutrient; would inhibit microbial growth. Likely FooDB annotation artifact.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin from plants (parsley/citrus family); biologically implausible in a fermented dairy matrix. FooDB predicted-not-measured.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace aroma aldehyde, not a growth nutrient; FooDB predicted flavour compound, negligible for a growth medium.

remove.name: (+/-)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Redundant non-proteinogenic diastereomer artifact of L-isoleucine already captured; not a distinct nutrient.

missing_added: lactic acid (dominant organic acid / major carbon source, was absent)
citric acid (partially fermented residual, was absent)
acetic acid (Leuconostoc citrate co-metabolite, was absent)
orotic acid (characteristic bovine-milk acid, was absent)
pyruvic acid, succinic acid (trace fermentation intermediates)
lactose amount + D-galactose + D-glucose (specific sugars; current profile had generic sugars but no lactose amount)
keep_uncovered: Myristoleic acid (C14:1, genuine minor milk fatty acid)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170874 (Milk, buttermilk, fluid, cultured, lowfat)

Gebreselassie et al. 2016, Chemical composition of naturally fermented buttermilk, Int. J. Dairy Technology, doi:10.1111/1471-0307.12236 (organic acids: lactic 5.35-11.56 mg/g, citric 52-1712 ug/g, plus acetic/orotic/pyruvic/succinic detected)
McCance & Widdowson / FRIDA dairy reference values (chloride, iodine)
notes: Cultured buttermilk is a fermented dairy medium: the DOMINANT carbon/energy source is lactic acid (~0.8 g/100g, ~89 mM) produced from lactose, with residual lactose (~5 g/100g, ~146 mM) the second major fermentable substrate; citrate is largely fermented to acetate/CO2/diacetyl. Rich in casein/whey amino acids (glutamate highest), Ca/K/P, riboflavin and B12. Added the previously-missing organic acids (the key gap) and amino acids Val/Phe; removed a preservative (epsilon-polylysine), a plant furanocoumarin, an aroma aldehyde, and a redundant isoleucine diastereomer. Cholesterol and short/medium-chain milk fatty acids are genuine dairy components and retained.
